query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- DIV112 Lesson 1: The Person of Christ (360036)
(360036, 'The hypostatic union refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Two persons in Christ","The union of divine and human natures in one person","Christ as only divine","Christ as only human"]', 'The union of divine and human natures in one person', 1),
(360036, 'The Council of Chalcedon (451 AD) affirmed Christ is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only God","Only man","Truly God and truly man in one person","A created being"]', 'Truly God and truly man in one person', 2),
(360036, 'Docetism is the heresy that Christ:', 'multiple_choice', '["Was only human","Only appeared to be human","Had two persons","Was created"]', 'Only appeared to be human', 3),
(360036, 'The term Logos in John 1 refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["A book","The Word - Christ as divine","Human reason only","Greek philosophy only"]', 'The Word - Christ as divine', 4),
(360036, 'Kenosis in Philippians 2 describes Christ:', 'multiple_choice', '["Gaining power","Emptying or humbling Himself","Losing divinity","Becoming an angel"]', 'Emptying or humbling Himself', 5),
(360036, 'The virgin birth is important because it:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is optional doctrine","Affirms Christs unique divine-human nature","Is merely symbolic","Has no theological significance"]', 'Affirms Christs unique divine-human nature', 6),
(360036, 'Arianism taught that Christ was:', 'multiple_choice', '["Fully God","A created being, not eternal God","Fully human only","An illusion"]', 'A created being, not eternal God', 7),
(360036, 'The communicatio idiomatum refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Communication between believers","The sharing of attributes between Christs natures","Prayer language","Spiritual gifts"]', 'The sharing of attributes between Christs natures', 8),
(360036, 'Jesus claim I AM in John 8:58 asserts His:', 'multiple_choice', '["Humanity only","Eternal divine existence","Created nature","Prophetic role only"]', 'Eternal divine existence', 9),
(360036, 'The impeccability of Christ means He:', 'multiple_choice', '["Could sin but chose not to","Was unable to sin","Was tempted to sin","Sinned occasionally"]', 'Was unable to sin', 10),
(360036, 'Explain the significance of the hypostatic union for Christian faith and salvation.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The hypostatic union is essential because only a divine-human Savior could bridge the gap between God and humanity.', 11),

-- DIV112 Lesson 2: The Work of Christ (360037)
(360037, 'Propitiation means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Example setting","Satisfaction of Gods wrath","Moral influence","Ransom to Satan"]', 'Satisfaction of Gods wrath', 1),
(360037, 'The penal substitutionary view of atonement teaches:', 'multiple_choice', '["Christ was an example only","Christ bore the penalty for our sins in our place","Salvation is by works","God ignores sin"]', 'Christ bore the penalty for our sins in our place', 2),
(360037, 'Christs threefold office includes Prophet, Priest, and:', 'multiple_choice', '["Apostle","King","Angel","Teacher"]', 'King', 3),
(360037, 'Reconciliation in atonement addresses:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only human guilt","The broken relationship between God and humanity","Physical healing only","Financial debt"]', 'The broken relationship between God and humanity', 4),
(360037, 'The resurrection of Christ demonstrates:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing significant","Victory over sin and death, vindication of His claims","He was only a prophet","His teachings were wrong"]', 'Victory over sin and death, vindication of His claims', 5),
(360037, 'Redemption imagery comes from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Modern banking","The slave market - buying back","Sports competitions","Political elections"]', 'The slave market - buying back', 6),
(360037, 'Christs ascension means He:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ceased to exist","Returned to heavenly glory and intercedes for believers","Lost His humanity","Became an angel"]', 'Returned to heavenly glory and intercedes for believers', 7),
(360037, 'The Christus Victor view emphasizes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Moral example","Christs victory over Satan and evil powers","Payment to the devil","Human effort"]', 'Christs victory over Satan and evil powers', 8),
(360037, 'Justification through Christs work means believers are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Made sinless in practice","Declared righteous before God","Earning salvation","Partially forgiven"]', 'Declared righteous before God', 9),
(360037, 'The blood of Christ symbolizes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Physical blood only","His sacrificial death and life given for atonement","A metaphor with no meaning","Old Testament rituals only"]', 'His sacrificial death and life given for atonement', 10),
(360037, 'Discuss the various atonement theories and explain which best captures the biblical teaching.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The penal substitutionary view best captures biblical teaching while other views provide complementary insights.', 11),

-- DIV113 Lesson 1: Modern Theological Movements (360041)
(360041, 'Liberal theology is characterized by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Strict biblical inerrancy","Accommodation to modern thought and higher criticism","Fundamentalist views","Rejection of all scholarship"]', 'Accommodation to modern thought and higher criticism', 2),
(360041, 'Neo-orthodoxy, associated with Karl Barth, emphasized:', 'multiple_choice', '["Human reason alone","The transcendence of God and encounter through the Word","Liberal theology","Fundamentalism"]', 'The transcendence of God and encounter through the Word', 3),
(360041, 'Liberation theology focuses primarily on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Individual salvation only","Social justice and liberation of the oppressed","Prosperity gospel","Withdrawal from society"]', 'Social justice and liberation of the oppressed', 4),
(360041, 'Process theology views God as:', 'multiple_choice', '["Unchanging and sovereign","Changing and developing with creation","Non-existent","Distant and uninvolved"]', 'Changing and developing with creation', 5),
(360041, 'Open theism challenges traditional views of:', 'multiple_choice', '["The Trinity","Gods exhaustive foreknowledge","Christs deity","Biblical authority"]', 'Gods exhaustive foreknowledge', 6),
(360041, 'Feminist theology critiques:', 'multiple_choice', '["All theology","Patriarchal interpretations and structures","Biblical authority entirely","Womens roles in any capacity"]', 'Patriarchal interpretations and structures', 7),
(360041, 'Postmodern theology tends to reject:', 'multiple_choice', '["All truth claims","Metanarratives and absolute truth claims","Local narratives","Personal experience"]', 'Metanarratives and absolute truth claims', 8),
(360041, 'The emergent church movement emphasizes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Traditional liturgy","Conversation, community, and cultural engagement","Isolation from culture","Rigid doctrine"]', 'Conversation, community, and cultural engagement', 9),
(360041, 'Evangelical theology maintains commitment to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Liberal scholarship","Biblical authority, the gospel, and personal conversion","Cultural accommodation","Rejection of scholarship"]', 'Biblical authority, the gospel, and personal conversion', 10),
(360041, 'What is the primary concern of contemporary theological movements?', 'multiple_choice', '["Irrelevance","Engaging modern questions while maintaining or revising tradition","Ignoring culture","Academic isolation"]', 'Engaging modern questions while maintaining or revising tradition', 1),
(360041, 'Evaluate one contemporary theological movement and assess its strengths and weaknesses from a biblical perspective.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Each theological movement has insights to offer but must be evaluated against biblical authority and orthodox doctrine.', 11);
